question: Write a function template `getLarger(T val1, T val2)` that accepts two arguments of the same comparable type `T` and returns the larger of the two. Demonstrate this template function with at least two different data types (e.g., `int` and `double`).

test_cases.expected: The output will be either "apple" or "banana" depending on the memory addresses of the string literals, as `getLarger` compares pointers, not string content.
The output will be either "zebra" or "aardvark" depending on the memory addresses of the string literals, as `getLarger` compares pointers, not string content.

errors: The function template `getLarger` compares pointer addresses instead of the pointed-to values when `T` is a C-style string (`char*` or `const char*`), leading to unexpected results for string content comparison. This can be misleading for users expecting lexicographical comparison.
